title: phlox glaberrima var. triflora
intro: Phlox glaberrima var. triflora, also known as three-flower phlox, is a type of flowering plant in the phlox family, native to the eastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and soothing digestive problems. The herbal preparation of Phlox glaberrima var. triflora is typically made into a tea, infusion, or tincture, although I can't verify its exact preparation methods.
